steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content:
The sentence contains two independent clauses that must be properly connected: “the exhibition set out to provide an overview of photography by women in the 1920s through the 1950s” and “its efforts were largely successful”.

The second clause follows a participial phrase: “given the collection's breadth of more than 120 photos”.

steps.title: Match the comma-conjunction rule
steps.content:
When a coordinating conjunction like “and” joins two independent clauses, a comma must come before it.

Choice D correctly uses “1950s, and”, following this rule.

Choices A, B, and C all fail:
  • A (1950s,): a comma alone between two independent clauses creates a comma splice. ✗
  • B (1950s): no punctuation at all creates a run-on sentence. ✗
  • C (1950s and): a coordinating conjunction joining two independent clauses requires a preceding comma. ✗
That matches choice D — D is correct.
